channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: ​At Least 10 Killed in South Korean Factory Fire
item.description: Survivors jumped from windows to escape the blaze, the deadliest at a factory in South Korea in two years. Four people are still missing.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:description: Firefighters spraying water to extinguish a fire at a factory in Daejeon, South Korea, on Friday.

item.title: A Public Holiday Every Week? Sri Lankans Bemoan the Enforced Day Off.
item.description: The island’s government, rationing fuel amid shortages from the Iran war, surprised residents by imposing an unusual four-day week.
item.dc:creator: Pamodi Waravita and Anupreeta Das
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vendor waiting for customers at a railway station in Colombo, Sri Lanka, on Wednesday, after the government announced a weekly holiday on Wednesdays to save fuel.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Is Considering ‘Winding Down’ Iran War
item.description: The president made the comment on social media a short time after telling reporters on the White House lawn that he wasn’t interested in a cease-fire because the U.S. was “obliterating the other side.” The Treasury Department lifted sanctions on some Iranian oil.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: On a Holiday of Renewal, Iranians Are Mourning and Fearful
item.description: As they start a new year, Iranians are reckoning with bombardment, repression and economic misery. Still, many are holding fast to ancient traditions.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Trump Organization Eyes Transylvania Golf Course Deal
item.description: The president’s company is eyeing a site for a golf course and luxury apartments. Will a huge landfill and a troubled project history stand in the way?
item.dc:creator: Rebecca R. Ruiz and Andrei Petre
item.media:credit: Andreea Campeanu for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Trump Organization is considering a real estate project in Romania that would be near a landfill and a makeshift settlement known as Pata Rat.

item.title: Seeking to Rely Less on China, U.S. Pushes a Rare Earths Partnership on a Reluctant Brazil
item.description: Seeking to reduce its reliance on China, the United States is pushing for a critical minerals deal with Brazil. The South American country is less eager.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Ju Faddul
item.media:description: A mine operated by Serra Verde Mining in Goiás state, Brazil, in July.

item.title: A Diary of War From an Unlikely Author: the Son of Iran’s President
item.description: In an online journal, Yousef Pezeshkian offers readers a mix of personal anecdotes and glimpses behind the scenes as Iranian leaders are picked off one after another.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A photo released by Iranian state media shows President Masoud Pezeshkian, center, with his son Yousef Pezeshkian, in blue jacket, in Tehran last month, before the war began.

item.title: U.S. Asked to Keep Military Planes in Sri Lanka Before Iran Airstrikes
item.description: Sri Lanka did not agree to the request, officials said, and has tried to remain neutral in the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Pamodi Waravita
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A U.S. Air Force bomber jet in southwest England this month.

item.title: Hamas Considering Disarmament Plan From Trump’s Board of Peace, Officials Say
item.description: The proposal envisions a phased process, in which the militant group would hand over its weapons and Israeli troops would withdraw. Hamas is expected to respond in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Natan Odenheimer, Aaron Boxerman and David M. Halbfinger
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hamas fighters in Nuseirat, Gaza, in February last year.

item.title: Denmark Was Ready to Blow Up Airfields to Stop a U.S. Invasion of Greenland
item.description: The Danes brought blood supplies, explosives and live ammunition to Greenland as part of contingency plans in case President Trump acted on his threats to seize the island.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Christopher F. Schuetze and Maya Tekeli
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: According to a report in Danish public media, Nuuk Airport in Greenland was one of two airports that the Danish military was planning to disable in the event of a U.S. invasion.

item.title: BTS Leans Into Korean History With ‘Arirang.’ Here Are the Major References.
item.description: The group’s new album “Arirang” includes a nod to a beloved Korean folk song and other important historical references.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.media:description: Trucks promoting the BTS comeback concert passing by the statue of King Sejong at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, on Thursday. The group performs at the square on Saturday.

item.title: Iran War Fallout: Southeast Asia Hard Hit by Skyrocketing Fuel Prices
item.description: Across Southeast Asia, a region heavily dependent on energy exports brought via the Strait of Hormuz, lives are being upended by higher oil and gas prices.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Jason Gutierrez, Muktita Suhartono, Kittiphum Sringammuang and Tung Ngo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fishermen on their boats while docked and unable to go out to sea due to diesel shortages and high prices, in Samut Sakhon, Thailand, on Monday.

item.title: A Meningitis Outbreak at the University of Kent Evokes Covid Memories
item.description: Residents and students in Canterbury, where the outbreak was centered, said measures to contain the disease were both familiar and frightening.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.media:description: Lining up for vaccines at the University of Kent in southeastern England on Wednesday. Two people have died and at least 29 others are believed to have been infected in the meningitis outbreak.

item.title: Lukashenko Jailed Her in Belarus, but She Wants the World to Talk to Him
item.description: Maria Kalesnikava is campaigning for the West to engage with the regime in Belarus that imprisoned her for more than five years.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Maria Kalesnikava with her flute this week in Berlin, where she now lives in exile.

item.title: Iran War Underscores the Diverging Aims of Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: The United States views Iran through a prism of global responsibilities and strategic goals. Israel has a more regional approach. After nearly three weeks of war, their paths are diverging.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Refineries serving the South Pars gas field in Asaluyeh, Iran, in 2019. The facilities, which process most of Iran’s natural gas for domestic consumption, were damaged in an attack on Wednesday.

item.title: Using Charm and Restraint, Japan’s Leader Mostly Avoids Trump’s Wrath
item.description: During her first visit to the White House, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi drew praise from President Trump. But the war in the Middle East will test their relationship.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ms. Takaichi and President Trump in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Chelsea Chief Under Abramovich Could Face Scrutiny Over Soccer Cheating
item.description: A former key aide to the Russian oligarch would face an investigation into her time overseeing the Premier League club should she return to soccer.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Marina Granovskaia before a match at Wembley Stadium in London in 2022, when she was a Chelsea board member.

item.title: Trump Signals Retreat From Goal of Regime Change in Iran
item.description: In the first days of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran, Mr. Trump pressed for a popular uprising against Tehran’s government.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People at an apartment building that was hit by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran last week.

item.title: The Road to the Battle of Hastings in 1066? Maybe Not a Road After All.
item.description: New research suggests that a legendary 200-mile march by Harold Godwinson, the last Anglo-Saxon king of England, may have actually involved some ships.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: Harold II, King of England (Harold Godwinson)
item.media:description: Re-enactors setting out from Clifford’s Tower in York, England, to follow the supposed journey of King Harold to the site of the Battle of Hastings, for the 950th anniversary of the battle in 2016.

item.title: U.S. Pauses Sanctions on Some Iranian Oil to Get More to Market
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent estimated that the move would add about 140 million barrels of crude to the oil market.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a post on X that Iran would see little economic benefit from the temporary removal of the sanctions.

item.title: A Look at the Escalating Battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: There are several ways the United States could attempt to reopen the waterway, all of which carry substantial risks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The village of Kumzar, along the Strait of Hormuz in Oman.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in Iran in Its Third Week
item.description: The Persian new year and the end of the holy month of Ramadan were marked by continuing strikes in the region as the war reached the end of its third week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr at the Mohammad Al-Amin Mosque in Beirut, Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iranian Targets to Try to Open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Israeli military said it had killed the spokesman for Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, and Iran warned that it could target American and Israeli military personnel.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Eric Schmitt, Francesca Regalado and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz to most Western ships, helping to drive up oil prices and rocking the global economy.

item.title: Switzerland Blocks Arms Sales to U.S. Over Iran War
item.description: Swiss law prohibits selling war materiel to countries involved in armed conflict, a policy the country has already cited to block sales to Israel and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The United States is one of the biggest markets for Swiss weapons exports, including small arms.

item.title: U.K. Allows U.S. to Use Bases to Hit Iranian Forces Menacing Strait Traffic
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has vowed not to be dragged into the war with Iran, but his government described its new position as essentially defensive.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A B-1 bomber at a Royal Air Force base in Fairford, England, on Sunday.

item.title: Energy facilities have been a potent target in the war.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Elliott, Blacki Migliozzi, Lazaro Gamio, Christiaan Triebert and Emmett Lindner

item.title: Stone Fragment Is Found in Wreck of Ship That Carried Parthenon Marbles
item.description: Experts said the fragment, recovered by divers from the Greek culture ministry, matched the style and dimensions of the Parthenon, but that it was too soon to be certain of its provenance.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai
item.media:description: Divers have found a marble fragment from the wreck of the Mentor, a ship that sank in 1802 while carrying Greek antiquities.

item.title: Aid Ship Departs for Cuba as Island Grapples With a Fuel Blockade
item.description: The “Nuestra América” humanitarian convoy plans to deliver more than 20 tons of critical supplies to Cuba. Some Cuban exiles view it with suspicion.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Paul Antoine Matos
item.media:description: People watching the sunset in Havana on Monday during a blackout.

item.title: A Few Ships Are Trickling Through the Strait of Hormuz With Iran’s Approval
item.description: Countries and companies have been negotiating with the Iranian authorities to secure passage. Some patterns are emerging.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing toward the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of the United Arab Emirates on Thursday.

item.title: Shigeaki Mori, Hiroshima Survivor Photographed With Obama, Dies at 88
item.description: He lived through the first atomic bombing in Japan and then spent decades researching the identities of 12 American P.O.W.s killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Shigeaki Mori with President Barack Obama in 2016. The first American president to visit Hiroshima, Mr. Obama paid tribute to Mr. Mori, “a man who sought out families of Americans killed here, because he believed their loss was equal to his own.”

item.title: Trump Officials Bypass Congress to Sell Weapons to U.A.E., Kuwait and Jordan
item.description: Some of the proposed sales, valued at more than $23 billion, were under review, while others were never sent to Congress. The administration is pushing them through without congressional approval.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio determined that “an emergency exists that requires the immediate sale” of weapons to the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Jordan, according to his department.

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader Issues a Statement but Remains Unseen
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has remained out of the public’s sight since assuming the post, amid reports that he was badly wounded.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei succeeded his father as Iran’s supreme leader earlier this month but has not appeared in public or on video since then.

item.title: U.S. Dispatches Marines and Warships to Middle East
item.description: Officials said 2,500 Marines from 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit in California and the U.S.S. Boxer amphibious ready group will go in April to relieve Marines already deployed in the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: A Marine Expeditionary Unit during training in Okinawa, Japan, last year. The unit is deployed in the Middle East.

item.title: NATO Pulls Last Military Trainers From Iraq Amid Strikes From Iran
item.description: The NATO mission, created in 2018, trained and advised Iraqi forces. Iraq is among the countries facing retaliatory attacks from the U.S.-Israeli war.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Air Force Gen. Alexus G. Grynkewich, NATO’s top commander, speaks during an armed services hearing, in March.

item.title: War-Weary Lebanon Marks Eid With Muted Celebration
item.description: For families displaced by Israeli airstrikes, the joy that usually marks the end of Ramadan has been replaced by uncertainty and hardship.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Diego Ibarra Sanchez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: In Photos: The War in Ukraine, After 4 Years
item.description: A photographic chronicle of Russia’s invasion.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Oksana Parafeniuk for The New York Times
item.media:description: A soldier who was thought to be dead after an identification mix-up visiting the site of his own grave in the Lviv region of western Ukraine in March.

item.title: Iran Executes 3 Men, Including Teenage Wrestler Saleh Mohammadi
item.description: The Iranian authorities accused the men of killing two police officers during anti-government protests in January. Human rights groups had raised concerns about the cases against them.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.media:description: Demonstrators in Tehran, in January. Protests triggered by soaring inflation and the collapse of the rial later expanded into broader demands for political change in Iran.

item.title: What To Know About the BTS Comeback Concert Streaming Live on Netflix
item.description: The K-pop supergroup’s first show in over three years will stream live on Netflix at 7 a.m. Eastern time on Saturday. The New York Times will cover it live from Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul on Saturday, hours before the BTS comeback concert.

item.title: New Data Shows Where ICE Has Been Most Active This Year
item.description: The pace of ICE arrests nationwide has topped 1,100 per day on average in 2026, but the rate of arrests has varied across the country in sometimes surprising ways.
item.dc:creator: Albert Sun, Allison McCann and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: How Geopolitics Threaten K-Pop’s Ambitions in China
item.description: Japanese members of some K-pop bands have been absent from recent concerts in China. Experts say that’s linked to tension between Tokyo and Beijing.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: Shotaro, third left, at a Riize concert in Austin, Texas, last year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Alan Rappeport, Emmett Lindner and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: How Japan Reacted to Trump’s Pearl Harbor Joke
item.description: Some people criticized President Trump’s decision to invoke a painful chapter of history. Others worried it might harm U.S.-Japan relations.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Hisako Ueno
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump met with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan in the Oval Office on Thursday.

item.title: Oil Prices Tick Up and U.S. Stocks Fall Sharply
item.description: Oil prices had been gyrating this week, after a new round of attacks on major energy facilities in Iran and Qatar raised concerns about energy supplies.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Spain Says the Sun Shields It From Rising Gas Costs. Is That True?
item.description: Pedro Sánchez, Spain’s prime minister, said the nation’s renewable energy system has softened the financial fallout from the war in Iran. The story is more complex.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Sheep grazing on a pasture next to solar panels, following a major blackout on the Iberian Peninsula, in the village of Hostalric, near Girona, Spain, last year.

item.title: How the Iran War Narrowed Flight Corridors Between Europe and Asia
item.description: One of the few paths left between the two continents threads through Azerbaijan, Georgia and Turkey, as global conflicts complicate aviation logistics.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Planes parked at Dubai International Airport on Wednesday, as airspace closures across the Middle East disrupted flight routes.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: Strikes continued in the region as attacks on energy infrastructure rattled global markets.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil refinery on Thursday in Haifa, Israel, that was damaged in an attack.

item.title: On Joe Rogan, Pierre Poilievre Talks Trump, Canada Tariffs and Bruce Lee
item.description: Floundering in the polls, Pierre Poilievre, the leader of Canada’s Conservatives, sat down with the popular podcaster in hopes of raising his standing at home and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: Pierre Poilievre, leader of Canada’s Conservative party, sat for a two-and-a-half-hour interview with Joe Rogan that was released on Thursday.

item.title: Iran Combines Real-World Missile Attacks With Online Threats
item.description: Iran launched a missile strike, a disinformation push and a cyberattack targeting Israel all at the same time, analysts say.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Inspecting the aftermath of an Iranian strike in southern Israel on Thursday. Iran also pushed disinformation messaging and launched a cyberattack against Israel on Thursday.

item.title: Is Russian Fuel Headed for Cuba, Testing the U.S. Blockade?
item.description: A Russian oil tanker is being closely tracked to see if it will challenge the Trump administration’s blockade on Cuba.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Jack Nicas
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Patriarch Ilia II, a Spiritual Symbol of Stability in Georgia, Dies at 93
item.description: The longest-serving leader in the history of the Georgian Orthodox Church, he helped guide his country in its transition from Soviet repression to modern statehood.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.media:description: Patriarch Ilia II before a Christmas service in Tblisi, Georgia’s capital, in January 2008. He provided a sense of continuity and stability during tense periods in the country after the collapse of the Soviet Union.

item.title: Trump Says He Won’t Send Troops to Iran but Leaves Wiggle Room
item.description: The president was cagey about his plans for Iran. He confirmed the Pentagon was seeking $200 billion to support a protracted war effort while also claiming it would be over soon.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Administration Faces Public Jitters as Oil Prices Surge Amid Iran War
item.description: President Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel suggested that the war with Iran could end soon, but that there were still more attacks ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm, David E. Sanger and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Trails and lights from missile defense interceptors are seen in the sky on Thursday, as excess gas is burned at an oil refinery in the northern Israel city of Haifa.

item.title: The Downfall of a Food Icon
item.description: We look at how Noma, the restaurant that revolutionized fine dining, became a byword for toxic kitchen culture.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Erik Refner for The New York Times

item.title: Trump’s Complaint About Israeli Strike on Gas Field Exposes Divergent Strategies
item.description: President Trump said he told Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel he disapproved of the attack, which sent energy markets reeling. But Israeli officials said the Americans were informed beforehand.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump has been trying to preserve Iran’s oil and gas infrastructure and keep the country from retaliating at energy facilities throughout the Persian Gulf.

item.title: ‘Africa Will Write Its Own History.’ Who Was Patrice Lumumba?
item.description: Remembering the African leader after a Brussels court this week ordered a retired Belgian diplomat stand trial for his 1961 assassination.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Patrice Lumumba in New York in July 1960. He helped liberate Congo from brutal Belgian rule in 1960 and became the nation’s first prime minister after independence.

item.title: U.S. Lifts Fertilizer Sanctions on Belarus as Iran War Causes Price Surge
item.description: The Trump administration made the move in exchange for Belarus’s freeing of 250 political prisoners, part of a rapprochement between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Tomas Dapkus
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Train cars carrying fertilizer ingredients from a Belarus factory in 2021. As global fertilizer prices soar, the U.S. has lifted sanctions on Belarus fertilizer products in exchange for the freeing of 250 political prisoners.

item.title: Jerusalem’s Old City sustains some damage after being largely spared in years of fighting.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: A roof was damaged by debris in East Jerusalem.

item.title: Trump Jokes About Pearl Harbor in Meeting With Japan’s Prime Minister
item.description: Breaking a taboo, President Trump needled Japan’s prime minister about the World War II attack, as she widened her eyes and appeared to take a deep breath in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández

item.title: U.S. Encourages Flow of Iranian Oil While It Battles Iran
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said removing sanctions on Iranian oil would lower global prices.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the sanctions exemption would apply to about 140 million barrels of Iranian oil that were currently at sea.

item.title: Death of University of Alabama Student in Barcelona Was Likely an Accident, Police Say
item.description: James Gracey, 20, a student at the University of Alabama, went missing during a visit to a beachfront nightclub in Barcelona. His body was found on Thursday, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk

item.title: U.K. Advisers Sent to U.S. to Help Develop Options to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The move comes after President Trump sharply criticized Prime Minister Keir Starmer for not supporting his initial military strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain said opening the Strait of Hormuz was “not a simple task.”

item.title: Israeli Officials Said U.S. Was Told About South Pars Attack
item.description: President Trump first said the United States “knew nothing” about an attack on the gas field in Iran, which sent global oil and gas prices soaring. He then said he cautioned Israel against it.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Refineries at the South Pars gas field in 2019.
